homeInfo.title: Audio Quality Evaluation
homeInfo.message:
Welcome to this audio captioning evaluation test.

You will listen to audio samples and evaluate how well a text caption describes each audio excerpt. For each sample, you will answer two questions using a slider scale.

The test is divided into two parts:

1. Example samples — A few practice samples to familiarize yourself with the task and the interface. Your answers on these will not be recorded.

2. Test samples — The actual evaluation. Please answer carefully.

After the test, you will be asked a short question about your musical background.

Please use headphones and ensure you are in a quiet environment.

Click the button below when you are ready to begin.

questions.name: Example sample 1 / 5
questions.description:
Listen to the audio sample below and answer both questions.

CAPTION:
questions.caption: The low quality recording features a didgeridoo melody. It sounds boomy and the recording is noisy and in mono.

0:
How correct is the description of the audio excerpt? Consider a correct caption to not have any information absent from the audio. Use the worst score if all the information in the caption is absent from the audio, and use the best score if all the information in the caption is present in the audio.

1 - completely incorrect
2 - mostly incorrect
3 - somewhat correct
4 - mostly correct
5 - completely correct
1:
How complete is the description of the audio excerpt? Consider a complete caption to have all relevant information concerning genre, mood, and instrumentation of the audio. Use the worst score if the caption does not have any relevant information on these aspects for the audio, and the best score if the caption contains all relevant information on these aspects for the audio.

1 - incomplete
2 - mostly incomplete
3 - somewhat complete
4 - mostly complete
5 - complete

questions.name: Example sample 3 / 5
questions.description:
Listen to the audio sample below and answer both questions.

CAPTION:
questions.caption: This song starts with a male narrator speaking a line. This is followed by a female voice singing the  main melody and each line is repeated by male and female voices. This song has a repetitive melody. This song has a call and response pattern. This is accompanied by an acoustic guitar playing chords and riffs at the end of lines. The percussion is played on a tabla. This song has a religious chant feel. This song can be played in a religious gathering.

questions.name: Ready?
questions.description:
The example samples are now complete.

The actual test will now begin. You will listen to audio samples and evaluate their captions, just as you did in the examples.

Click 'Start test' when you are ready.

questions.name: Test sample 8 / 10
questions.description:
Listen to the audio sample below and answer both questions.

CAPTION:
questions.caption: This track features a slow, ambient soundscape driven by ethereal choir vocals that create a hypnotic and meditative atmosphere. Although the metadata suggests various electronic subgenres, the prominent voice instrumentation and lack of spoken lyrics emphasize an immersive listening experience. The subtle rhythm supports these vocal layers to establish a truly captivating mood for the listener.

questions.name: About You
questions.description: Please tell us about yourself and your experience. This helps us interpret the results.

fields.label: How many years of musical experience do you have?
Do you play an instrument? If yes, which one(s)?
fields.placeholder: e.g. piano, guitar, drums — or leave empty if none

fields.label: What are you using to listen to the audio samples?
How would you describe your listening environment?
Have you participated in audio evaluation tests before?
Any comments or suggestions to improve the test?
fields.placeholder: e.g. The audio was too short, the scale was confusing, etc.
